title: Module 1
content:
Introduction to Design:  Definition, steps in design process, preferred
numbers, standards and codes in design. (1) Materials and their properties-
combined stresses, stress concentration factor (2)
Theories of Failure:  Guest’s Theory, Rankine’s Theory, St. Venant’s
Theory, Haigh’s Theory, and Von Mises and Hencky Theory. Shock and
impact loads, fatigue loading, endurance limit stress, factors affecting
endurance limit, factor of safety. (2)
Design of Shaft:  Shafting- material, design considerations, causes of failure
in shafts, design based on strength, rigidity and critical speed, design for static
and fatigue loads, repeated loading, reversed bending (6)

title: Module 2
content:
Bearings: Rolling contact bearing- Design of bearings, Types, Selection of a
bearing type, bearing life, static and dynamic load capacity, axial and radial
loads, selection of bearings, dynamic equivalent load.
Sliding contact bearing- lubrication, lubricants, viscosity, Journal bearings,
hydrodynamic theory, Sommerfield number, design considerations (6)
Springs: Classification, spring materials, stresses and deflection of helical
springs, axial loading, curvature effect, resilience, static and fatigue loading,
surging, critical frequency, end construction, design of leaf springs, design of
torsion bar (5)

title: Module 3
content:
Design of Gears: Classification, virtual or formative number of teeth, gear
tooth failures, Beam strength, Lewis equation, Buckingham’s equation for
dynamic load, wear load, endurance strength of tooth, surface durability, heat
dissipation, Design procedure of Spur gear (3)
Helical gears: - Terminology, Virtual or equivalent number of teeth, Tooth
proportions, Beam strength, and Wear strength of Helical gears, Design
procedure of Helical gear (2)
Bevel gears: - Classification, Terminology, Pitch angle for bevel gears,
Strength of bevel gear, beam strength, wear tooth load, Formative number of
teeth, Design procedure of Bevel gear (2)
Worm gears: - Characteristics of worm gears, Terminology, Advantages and
disadvantages, Applications, Terms in Worm gear, Strength of Worm gear,
Dynamic strength, Wear tooth load, Design procedure of Worm gear (2)

title: Module 4
content:
Design of Internal combustion engine parts:  Crankshaft, Piston, Cylinder,
Connecting rod, Flywheel, (5)
Clutches: friction clutches, design considerations, multiple disc clutches, cone
clutch, centrifugal clutch. (4)
Brakes: Internal expanding shoe brake, Disc brakes. (3)
